text: The Monad blockchain ecosystem is gaining attention as a high-performance, fully EVM-compatible, decentralized platform offering 10,000 TPS and ~0.5-1 second finality. It competes with other chains like Solana, Hyperliquid, MegaETH, Sui, and Ethereum L2s by avoiding trade-offs in speed, decentralization, developer familiarity, and UX. Monad is positioning itself as a versatile chain for DeFi, gaming, AI agents, and NFTs, with growing institutional appeal and ecosystem development.
author: elfa_2044356896337895904

text: Quantum computing blockchain project Quip Network has launched a testnet attracting over 15,000 users focused on real research and experimentation rather than hype. The network enables mining $QUIP tokens through optimization problems solved by classical and quantum hardware, aiming to create a decentralized marketplace for quantum compute and enhance post-quantum security for assets on chains like Ethereum and Solana.
author: elfa_2044385695259582571

text: Allbirds ($BIRD) announced a pivot from its footwear business to AI compute infrastructure, planning to rebrand as NewBird AI and focus on GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS). The stock surged over 200% intraday following the announcement, reflecting the strong market hype around AI. However, some analysts view this move skeptically, considering it a distressed company leveraging AI hype to extract remaining value, with significant execution risks ahead.
author: elfa_2044468052805173393

text: The crypto community is discussing the structural dynamics of crypto valuation, emphasizing that price movements are driven more by community belief and narrative than traditional financial metrics like earnings or P/E ratios. This is exemplified by projects like XRP, which benefits from regulatory clarity and institutional adoption prospects, and meme coins, which reflect the cultural transmission and belief systems underpinning crypto markets. The SPX6900 token is highlighted as a case study of a community-driven asset with a fixed supply, decentralized governance, and a strong, quantifiable mission, attracting institutional interest and demonstrating resilience through market cycles. This narrative challenges conventional valuation frameworks and underscores the importance of community conviction in crypto asset value.
author: elfa_2044390314954129880

text: Hyperliquid ($HYPE) is gaining significant traction in the crypto market, with its native token rallying strongly and approaching all-time highs. The platform leads in 24-hour fees among all chains, driven by high perpetual volumes rivaling major centralized exchanges. Upcoming protocol upgrades like HIP-3 and HIP-4 aim to expand asset offerings including non-crypto assets and improve market infrastructure, potentially boosting revenue and token demand. Hyperliquid's ecosystem includes integrations with projects like Unit Labs (@unitxyz and @tradexyz), which focus on decentralized perpetual trading of traditional financial assets, and the platform is seen as a key player in the 'house of all finance' narrative. Community sentiment is bullish, with expectations of $HYPE reaching triple-digit prices and growing user adoption. Events such as Hyperliquid meetups and ongoing development highlight active community engagement and ecosystem growth.

text: The Difference Between Regulated and Unregulated Stablecoins > A stablecoin is regulated when its issuer operates under the supervision of a licensed financial regulator and meets specific requirements for reserves, disclosure and consumer protection. > An unregulated stablecoin (USDT, for example) is one whose issuer operates without meaningful oversight of its reserve composition, issuance practices or redemption obligations. text: Closed BTC long @ 75.3k Played the cards right and got the additional fill at 73.5k. Overall great trade and made up for the profits I round tripped over the weekend. Now that Strc's bid is gone, BTC's dynamics moving forward will look quite different and we should expect it to react to events differently. Reason for close is I don't have a great read on where we trade over the coming days. Was expecting a slightly bigger move from Strc's final raise day but maybe it plays out like last month and Saylor's going to swap over multiple days for better execution? Going flat for now until I have a better read on where the next trend will form on LTF's. Right now direction feels like a coin flip, never good to be in positions if that's your basis. Seeing some recent news of physical escalation in the ME and foggy sentiment from ceasefire positioning. Even though I said people don't care about the war and its having diminishing returns, the fact there seems to be large aircraft carriers and underground missile reserves heading towards conflict zones is rather concerning. Will continue looking for catalysts with early execution. Trying to be extra patient as I'm starting to feel a little burnout from excessive screen time the past couple weeks. Not having a trade on can be a good mental reset if you've been trading in high volume and gives you a fresher perspective on direction, the skill is to be patient and not fire on low confidence positions.

text: From this week's The Decentralised: 'Aave will win'? Three of Aave DAO's longest-serving service providers have announced their departures from the collective, citing what they describe as a growing concentration of power around Aave Labs, the company run by protocol founder Stani Kulechov. The exits follow a landmark funding vote that will see the DAO provide Aave Labs with $25 million in stablecoins and 75,000 AAVE tokens, in exchange for the firm routing 100% of revenue from Aave-branded products back to the DAO treasury. Aave Chan Initiative founder Marc Zeller, one of the most prominent critics, argued that Aave Labs' funding request failed to meet the transparency standards expected of other service providers, and accused Labs employees of using their voting power to influence the outcome. Kulechov's response has done little to ease tensions. His post declaring "Aave will win" called for a "zero-bureaucracy" approach with "zero room for friction," a framing that sits uneasily with the open deliberation that DAO governance is supposed to embody. More from this week's top stories: → Roman Storm defends Tornado Cash operation as judge weighs acquittal → A hacker created $1.2bn of counterfeit crypto. They only sold it for $237,000 → Ethereum is abandoning layer 2s. Arbitrum's creator still sees a bright future ahead From the land of DAOs: → VOTE: Aave DAO to decide if it will axe Aave V3 on Scroll blockchain → VOTE: Sky DAO votes to launch new cross-chain bridge for Avalanche blockchain → VOTE: Across DAO greenlights transition from DAO to US-based C corporation [FEATURE] Worried about how quantum computing might affect the blockchain? Worry no more: Octra, an L1 blockchain native to Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) represents the next stage of cryptography. Find out more about Octra and their token sale via Uniswap CCA. Get The Decentralised delivered to your inbox every week.

text: We were given the opportunity to test Plasma One's global stablecoin neobank A sneak peak below of what they have to offer! Plasma One combines: • a clean cash balance + card layer (Apple Pay integration available) • built-in yield on idle stablecoin balances • rewards and your usual referral mechanisms • most importantly, a simple app flow that actually feels consumer-ready Feels a product that makes crypto feel like a modern money app (both the onboarding and navigation were smooth) Some extra thoughts: • card + earn + rewards is great and a step-up in terms of innovation and making crypto actually usable IRL, but a lot of fintech and crypto apps also do have the same same version of that > so the biggest question is why use them what is their moat and distribution? Will wait for the team to reveal more details on their X factor edge • what made some of the other card providers particularly lucrative (sustainability aside) was the perks that they gave (crypto.com card with Spotify and Netflix perks, EtherFi with cashbacks). Plasma should also have a compelling USP to differentiate from the crowd • granted the product is still in demo, and hence why it still feels a bit empty. The UI is clean, but clean alone is not enough to win. Again, it is early days and Plasma should have more integrations down the road • the yields probably need a more in-depth explanation of where it comes from and what the risks are, being transparent gains trust from users • traditional fintech multi-currency cards allow users a holistic view of fees and FX rates - we think this is missing in crypto cards and something that players could improve on

text: Bitcoin researchers propose BIP-361 to freeze quantum vulnerable wallets + phased sunset of legacy signatures BIP-361 proposes a staged Bitcoin migration away from current legacy signatures toward post-quantum-safe ones. First it would stop sending to quantum-vulnerable addresses, then after a long notice period it would make old ECDSA/Schnorr spends invalid, effectively forcing holders, wallets, exchanges, and custodians to migrate before a deadline Quantum computing progress could eventually threaten Bitcoin UTXOs with exposed public keys, so the goal is to reduce the attack surface early and give the ecosystem a clear migration timeline instead of waiting for an emergency

text: QCP Market Colour, 15 April 2026 BTC Rallies on Relief, But Markets Are Not Buying Resolution BTC joined the overnight relief rally, pushing back toward the mid-$74,000 area as markets reacted to reports of a preliminary US-Iran framework. Equities bounced, oil fell, and crypto caught a bid. But the bigger signal was what did not confirm the move: long-end yields barely moved, gold held firm, and rates did not price a more meaningful inflation relief trade. That suggests this is headline relief, not full resolution. The reason is enrichment. Iran is at 60% enriched uranium, while the US wants below 20%. That gap is not closed by a framework headline, and Tehran has not signalled a willingness to make that concession. Markets may be trading the ceasefire angle, but the core risk remains unresolved. In crypto, BTC spot is grinding higher despite negative funding and softer open interest, suggesting shorts are still leaning against the move. But options remain cautious. Front-end vol is still muted, 1-month vol remains below 3-month, and 30-day 25-delta risk reversals still show better demand for downside protection than upside exposure. This still looks like a spot-led relief rally, not a broader re-risking. The broader macro regime has not changed. The Fed remains boxed in, liquidity is still tight, and this is a geopolitical bounce rather than a regime shift. Last week was about fading the blockade. This week is about whether to fade the relief. Read more:
